title: ABAC Policy Decision Point Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the abac policy decision point capability, derived from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage and not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facets cite verified manifest source rows as lineage; proof is hermetic/stale and not current readiness. Do not treat as implementation authority or fresh proof; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The decision point reads the current resource policy, audits, and sets a replacement, while request evaluation is authorization-server-first when a token is present, falls back to a static permission backstop when the resource is unregistered or the server is unavailable, denies fail-closed on policy denial, and requests step-up on insufficient auth level. Policy administration and per-request evaluation are distinct flows over the same guard metadata.

stateModel.description: State is each guarded route's resource, scope, and required permission, plus optional resource-policy records and a decision result that is one of granted, policy-denied, insufficient-auth-level, server-unavailable, or resource-not-registered, with a static permission fallback. The decision is derived per request rather than stored.

permissions.description: Routes declare an explicit required permission, resource, and scope consumed as a policy-enforcement point, and resource-policy writes sit behind tenant auth-settings permission with audit-before-adapter mutation. Authorization itself is the permission gate for every protected operation.

contracts.description: The operation surface is resource-policy read and replacement plus the per-route resource and scope guard metadata consumed by the request pipeline and the query authorizer. Decisions are returned to callers rather than persisted as contract output.

validation.description: Unknown or unregistered resources degrade only to the static permission backstop, an absent static permission denies, an injected resource policy may allow without a static permission, and wildcard policy names are accepted. Validation never opens an unregistered resource by default.

errorModel.description: Policy denial maps to 403, insufficient auth level maps to a step-up-required/401, a missing expected token maps to authentication-required/401, and static fallback without a permission maps to 403; every path is fail-closed by default. Server unavailability is an explicit non-decisive degradation, not an implicit allow.

auditModel.description: Resource-policy mutations emit an audit-before-change event and audit failure prevents the adapter mutation, while read-only authorization checks are logged or returned rather than audited per request. Audit precedes any policy change.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is invariant-ready for the local policy seam, and authorization-server unavailability and resource-not-registered are explicit degraded, non-decisive states with a static fallback rather than a fake allow. Degraded mode keeps read-only readiness and audit visible.

proof.description: Unit proofs cover the decision logic and resource-policy administration and a runtime proof covers permission-before-entitlement policy ordering, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic state/policy-chain validation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
